title: CachingStore never holds Mutex across .await — correct and should be defended
description: CachingStore uses tokio::sync::Mutex but drops the guard before every .await point (explicit block scopes in get() and refresh()). This is the correct discipline: holding a tokio Mutex across .await causes deadlock when all executor threads are blocked waiting on each other. A contributor might suggest using std::sync::Mutex instead; that would work but would panic if the guard is held across an .await (Rust does not prevent this). The tokio Mutex with explicit block scopes is the correct production pattern. The module-level doc comment explicitly documents this invariant.
